summary: Verify the first official-source OpenSCAD WebAssembly artifact byte-for-byte, record its hosted identity and checksums, and prevent evidence-only ENGINE_VERSION edits from launching unchanged 90-minute builds.
specSections: Section 2.7/A-5 official-source engine build and artifact checksums
FR-4.7 separately fetched and cached WebAssembly artifact
AC-4.a same-build native and web parity

externalInputs.purpose: Obtain exact-SHA hosted build status and artifact 8374641324 for independent manifest, byte, bootstrap, API, and side-asset inspection.

decisions.topic: Verified source-built WASM artifact and build trigger scope
decisions.decision: Record the exact run, artifact, manifest, JavaScript, and WebAssembly identities in ENGINE_VERSION; retain only the workflow file as the automatic build trigger because it contains every byte-affecting source, container, toolchain, and command input while ENGINE_VERSION is separately validated in regular CI.
decisions.rationale: Hosted run 29495964243 produced the only two generated runtime assets, and independent hashes match its manifest. ENGINE_VERSION now contains output evidence as well as approved pins, so treating every evidence-only metadata update as an engine input would spend 90 minutes rebuilding identical bytes. Pin drift still fails the production workflow contract, and a pin change necessarily updates the workflow before a build can pass.

testEvidence.criterion: The hosted artifact is bound to the approved source recipe and its retained bytes match the manifest
testEvidence.test: Inspect the exact-SHA hosted run and downloaded artifact outside the repository, enumerate every regular file, and calculate SHA-256 independently.

red.command: ENGINE_VERSION and hosted artifact inspection before run 29495964243 completed
red.outcome: RED: ENGINE_VERSION truthfully recorded that no source-built WASM artifact had yet been produced, so no source-build artifact checksum claim could pass.

green.command: gh run view 29495964243; gh run download 29495964243; Get-FileHash -Algorithm SHA256 on the manifest, openscad.js, and openscad.wasm
green.outcome: GREEN: the exact candidate run passed all five build steps. Artifact 8374641324 contains only a 100,027-byte JavaScript module and 10,760,714-byte WASM asset plus its 533-byte manifest; independently calculated hashes E458673D46D506D77B780C526D6E5492250F353D582057C6F912724A9586D86E and 64124667A8E54EAF20324A0B1BF707FC5E338D7C92187BB411982FE5F69EF82F match the manifest exactly.

testEvidence.criterion: Artifact-evidence metadata cannot launch an unchanged heavyweight engine build
testEvidence.test: Remove ENGINE_VERSION from the path trigger, keep exact production validation of its canonical pins in regular CI, and mutation-test reintroducing the metadata-only trigger.

red.command: Adversarial review of the successful workflow path filter before recording artifact output metadata
red.outcome: RED: changing wasm.build_status, run identity, artifact ID, sizes, or checksums in ENGINE_VERSION would have launched the 90-minute build even though none of those output-evidence fields can change produced bytes.

green.outcome: GREEN: 1 test file and 26 tests passed, including rejection of an added ENGINE_VERSION path trigger while retaining exact workflow and canonical ENGINE_VERSION pin validation.

questions: Q-0023 remains open only for whether the official prebuilt archive may substitute; it no longer blocks or describes the completed official-source build.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
